5th China CSS Developer Conference — Notes on Zhang Xinxu's Talk

On 2019-03-20 in Shenzhen, the CSS Developer Conference (hosted by W3C and W3ctech) took place. Footage is on YouTube. I worked through the demos as a practice exercise — these are my notes based on Zhang Xinxu's talk.
5th China CSS Developer Conference — Zhang Xinxu
Each section has a link at the end pointing to Zhang Xinxu's own site.

Mystery voice: I couldn't quite reproduce a clean parallelogram. Per Zhang Xinxu, browser support isn't fully there yet, so the result might still look off.
I'd never built a checker pattern in CSS, but Zhang Xinxu's trick is to layer two triangles with offsets.

background: linear-gradient(45deg,
  #eee 25%,
  transparent 25%,
  transparent 75%,
  #eee 75%),
linear-gradient(
  45deg,
  #eee 25%,
  transparent 25%,
  transparent 75%,
  #eee 75%),
linear-gradient(
  45deg,
  #eee 25%,
  transparent 25%,
  transparent 75%,
  #eee 75%);

This transition didn't show much effect for me, but apparently works on mobile. The animation is pretty cool though.
Nice effect — use CSS to flip the same image between day and night, useful for highly-repeated images.
This'd melt hearts — make photos dreamier with CSS. My photo choice wasn't ideal, so the effect doesn't show much.

After watching the CSS Developer Conference, I really got a sense of how powerful CSS has become. Some features still have spotty browser support, but I picked up a lot of techniques.
There are 2-3 demos I left out because the effects didn't reproduce well on my setup.
(Mystery voice: most of the later ones I just copy-pasted to test. Recreating purely from screenshots is really hard.)
